annotations.label: Taxicab number (1729)

anchor.snippet: It is the first nontrivial taxicab number

annotations.note: No taxicab-number definition or instance for 1729 found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ramanujan/Hardy–Ramanujan number

anchor.snippet: It is known as the Ramanujan number or Hardy–Ramanujan number

annotations.note: Grepping for `Ramanujan` only yields modular forms / Chudnovsky references, not a Hardy–Ramanujan number definition.

annotations.label: 1729 is composite, taxicab, and Carmichael

anchor.snippet: 1729 is a composite number, the first nontrivial taxicab number, and a Carmichael number

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Carmichael` is the reduced-totient function, not the Carmichael-number property; FermatPsp.lean explicitly notes Carmichael numbers are not yet defined.

annotations.label: Smallest absolute Euler pseudoprime

anchor.snippet: It is also the smallest absolute Euler pseudoprime

annotations.note: No Euler-pseudoprime or absolute-Euler-pseudoprime declaration exists in Mathlib (only Fermat pseudoprimes).

annotations.label: Dimension of Fourier transform for fast multiplication

anchor.snippet: 1729 is the dimension of the Fourier transform on which the fastest known algorithm for multiplying two numbers is based

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the Harvey–van der Hoeven multiplication algorithm or its 1729-dim Fourier transform.

annotations.label: Galactic algorithm example

anchor.snippet: This is an example of a galactic algorithm

annotations.note: No notion of 'galactic algorithm' is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Schiemann's quadratic form result

anchor.snippet: Schiemann found that such quadratic forms must be in four or more variables, and the least possible discriminant of a four-variable pair is 1729

annotations.note: No reference to Schiemann or this isospectral-quadratic-form result is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First Fermat near miss

anchor.snippet: 1729 is the first number in the sequence of "Fermat near misses"

annotations.note: The 'Fermat near miss' concept (a^3 + b^3 = c^3 ± 1) has no formalization in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Taxicab number definition

anchor.snippet: This conversation led to the definition of the taxicab number as the smallest integer that can be expressed as a sum of two positive cubes in a given number of distinct ways

annotations.note: No definition of taxicab number (smallest integer expressible as a sum of two positive cubes in n ways) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 1729 as second taxicab number
annotations.note: Without a taxicab-number definition in Mathlib, the statement that 1729 = Ta(2) is not formalized.
